Programme Deep-Dive

Our Work in Detail

Education & Life Skills

Quality education and school-support initiatives that nurture young minds and break cycles of intergenerational poverty in Mbale City and surrounding communities.

In Eastern Uganda, school dropout rates remain alarmingly high — particularly for girls, children with disabilities, and those from the most marginalised households. Many families cannot afford school materials, and over-stretched school systems leave at-risk learners behind.

HNG delivers targeted tutoring, mentorship, life-skills workshops, and school materials provision. Our after-school programme creates a safe, structured space where children thrive and build confidence for lifelong learning.

450+
Children in after-school
87%
Retention rate
12
Partner schools
200+
Kits distributed

Grassroots Sports & Talent Development

Rugby and community sports are used as powerful tools for discipline, teamwork, physical wellness, and holistic child development. HNG uses the playing field as a classroom.

Sport is one of the most powerful vehicles for change — yet for many children in Mbale, access to quality coaching, equipment, and safe playing spaces remains out of reach. Without mentors, raw talent goes unrecognised and undeveloped.

HNG partners with Uganda Rugby Union and community coaches to deliver structured training, inter-school leagues, and talent exposure events. We coach both athletic excellence and life values: respect, perseverance, and cooperation.

600+
Youth in sports
8
Schools covered
45%
Female participants
3
National players

Child Protection & Juvenile Justice

Advocacy for child rights, prevention of abuse and violence, and rehabilitation and counselling support for affected children and their caregivers.

Thousands of children across Eastern Uganda face violence, abuse, neglect, and exploitation. Juvenile justice systems are under-resourced and many at-risk youth lack access to counselling, legal aid, or meaningful rehabilitation pathways.

HNG works alongside local authorities, schools, and families to create safer environments for children. We run community awareness campaigns, provide psychosocial support, and advocate for systemic change at local and district levels.

300+
Children supported
18
Community campaigns
5
District partnerships
95%
Case follow-up rate

Girls Empowerment, Gender Equality & Disability Inclusion

Targeted outreach programmes, girls' camps, skills-building workshops, and livelihood training that promote gender equality and sustainable self-reliance.

Girls in Mbale face disproportionate barriers to education, sport, and economic participation. Early marriage, gender-based violence, and cultural norms limit opportunities for girls — while persons with disabilities remain largely excluded from mainstream development programmes.

HNG's Girls' Empowerment Programme delivers structured camps, mentorship from successful women leaders, financial literacy training, and livelihood skills. Our GESI policy ensures disability inclusion is mainstreamed across all activities.

380+
Girls enrolled
4
Annual camps held
33%
Women in leadership
60+
PWDs supported

Community Sustainability & Livelihood Innovation

Training, exposure to life-changing solutions, and collaborative community projects that address poverty, health, environment, and livelihood improvement.

Communities in Eastern Uganda face intersecting challenges: climate vulnerability, limited economic diversification, and limited access to market linkages for small-scale producers. Short-term interventions have failed to break the cycle of poverty.

HNG's sustainability programme integrates environmental awareness, climate-resilient agriculture, savings groups, and entrepreneurship training. We build community capacity for long-term self-reliance, not dependency.

250+
Households reached
6
Communities active
12
Savings groups
70%
Income improvement
5+ Years of Impact
About HNG

A Community Organisation
with Global Vision

Hope for the Next Generation is a registered not-for-profit, community-based organisation working at the intersection of grassroots sport, quality education, child protection, gender equality, and sustainable livelihoods in Mbale City, Eastern Uganda.

Founded by a group of dedicated community leaders, HNG operates under the NGO Act, 2016, and is governed by a democratically elected Board of Directors. We are driven by the conviction that every vulnerable child deserves dignity, opportunity, and a future worth hoping for.

👶

Child-Centred

Children's best interests guide every decision we make.

🤝

Integrity

Full financial transparency — openly published.

♿

Inclusion

Girls, persons with disabilities, and those in extreme poverty actively served.

📊

Accountability

Annual reports and audited financials publicly available.

🌍

Community Ownership

Communities are active participants — not passive recipients.

💡

Innovation

Creative, evidence-based solutions are our hallmark.

Leadership

Global Goals

Advancing the
Global Goals

HNG is formally committed to the United Nations 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. Our programmes directly advance four SDGs — providing the framework for accountability that institutional donors require.

4
📖
SDG 4 — Quality Education
HNG delivers educational support, life-skills training, and mentorship to hundreds of vulnerable children annually.
Our programmes tackle school dropout, support learners with disabilities, and equip youth with skills for lifelong learning. 450+ children in active after-school programming.
5
⚧️
SDG 5 — Gender Equality
Our Girls Empowerment Programme, women's livelihood workshops, and GESI-mainstreamed governance advance equality.
HNG targets one-third women's representation in leadership. 380+ girls enrolled in empowerment and life-skills programming.
13
🌿
SDG 13 — Climate Action
Our Community Sustainability Programme integrates environmental awareness and climate-resilient livelihood training.
6 communities equipped with sustainable agriculture techniques and climate adaptation skills for long-term community resilience.
16
⚖️
SDG 16 — Peace & Justice
HNG's Child Protection Programme advocates for children's rights and builds safe, accountable community institutions.
300+ at-risk children supported with counselling and rehabilitation. 18 community awareness campaigns conducted across Mbale City.
Our Commitment

How We Advance
Each Global Goal

HNG's commitment to the 2030 Agenda is embedded in our governance, planning, and reporting frameworks. Every programme is designed to deliver measurable contributions to the SDGs we have adopted.

📖

SDG 4 — Quality Education

Inclusive and equitable quality education

HNG delivers educational support, life-skills training, school materials provision, and structured mentorship to hundreds of vulnerable children annually. Our after-school programme creates safe spaces for learning and development, targeting children at highest risk of dropout — including girls, learners with disabilities, and those from the most marginalised households in Mbale City.

450+ children in after-school 87% retention rate 200+ school kits distributed
⚧️

SDG 5 — Gender Equality

Achieve gender equality and empower women

HNG's Girls' Empowerment Programme, women's livelihood workshops, girls' camps, and GESI-mainstreamed governance structure directly advance gender equality and women's empowerment. HNG targets one-third women's representation in all leadership positions, and our GESI Policy is publicly available for full accountability.

380+ girls enrolled 4 annual girls' camps 33% women in leadership
🌿

SDG 13 — Climate Action

Take urgent action to combat climate change

HNG's Community Sustainability Programme integrates environmental awareness, sustainable agriculture techniques, and climate-resilient livelihood training, equipping communities to understand and respond to climate change impacts. Working with smallholder farmers and youth groups across 6 communities, we build long-term environmental stewardship.

6 communities trained 12 savings groups 250+ households
⚖️

SDG 16 — Peace & Justice

Strong institutions and access to justice

HNG's Child Protection and Juvenile Justice Programme advocates for children's rights, supports rehabilitation of at-risk youth, promotes access to justice, and works to build safe, accountable community institutions. We collaborate with local authorities, schools, and families to prevent violence and create protective environments.

300+ children supported 18 campaigns run 5 district partnerships

"Contributing to a better world — together."

Why Donate to HNG?

📊
Full Transparency

Audited financials published annually. Every shilling accounted for.

📍
Community-Led

98% of staff are from Mbale — your money stays local.

📈

🌍

Volunteer Opportunities

Sports Coaching

Coach rugby and community sports sessions. Experience preferred but passion essential. Weekends, 8am–12pm.

Education Support

Tutoring and mentoring in after-school programmes. Any subject area, minimum commitment 4 hours/week.

Communications & Design

Photography, video, social media, graphic design, website. Remote-friendly. Flexible hours.

Fundraising & Partnerships

Help us connect with donors and corporate partners. Business development experience valuable.

Express Your Interest

We aim to respond within 3 working days.

Partner With HNG

We welcome partnerships with corporate organisations, international institutions, government bodies, and community stakeholders who share our commitment to transforming lives in Eastern Uganda.

Corporate Partnership

CSR alignment with SDG-accredited community programmes. Brand visibility across HNG's programmes and communications.

Institutional Funding

Multi-year grant partnerships with full M&E frameworks, audited reporting, and SDG impact measurement.

Community Partnership

In-kind support, venue provision, skills transfer, and community mobilisation partnerships with local organisations.

Partnership Enquiry
